title: Aadhaar New Enrollment How to Apply
content:
Aadhaar new enrollment how to apply get Aadhaar card
To get a new Aadhaar card visit an Aadhaar Enrollment Center.
Step 1: Find nearest enrollment center at https://appointments.uidai.gov.in/easearch.aspx
Step 2: Book an appointment online or walk in directly.
Step 3: Carry original documents for enrollment.
Step 4: Fill the enrollment form at the center.
Step 5: Give biometrics — 10 fingerprints, iris scan, and photograph.
Step 6: Collect the EID enrollment ID slip — a 14-digit number.
Step 7: Download e-Aadhaar after 90 days using EID at https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Cost: FREE. Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Documents Required Proof of Identity Address
content:
Aadhaar documents required needed proof of identity address date of birth
For new Aadhaar enrollment you need these documents:
Proof of Identity any one: Voter ID card, PAN card, Passport, Driving License, Government ID card, Bank passbook with photo.
Proof of Address any one: Electricity bill, Water bill, Gas bill, Bank passbook, Rent agreement, Passport, Voter ID, Property tax receipt.
Proof of Date of Birth any one: Birth certificate, 10th class marksheet, Passport, PAN card, School leaving certificate.
For Aadhaar address update online: Electricity bill, Bank passbook, Rent agreement, Driving License, Passport.
For Aadhaar name update: Gazette notification, Marriage certificate, School certificate.
For mobile number update: Visit enrollment center in person — no extra documents needed apart from Aadhaar.
All documents must be originals. Photocopies not accepted at enrollment centers.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Download e-Aadhaar Online PDF
content:
Aadhaar download e-Aadhaar online PDF how to get Aadhaar card online
Step 1: Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Click on Download Aadhaar.
Step 3: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number.
Step 4: Enter the OTP sent to your Aadhaar-registered mobile number.
Step 5: Click Download — the PDF will be saved to your device.
Password to open the downloaded Aadhaar PDF: First 4 letters of your name in CAPITAL followed by birth year.
Example: Name is Rahul born 1995 — password is RAHU1995.
Example: Name is Priya born 2000 — password is PRIY2000.
No registered mobile? Visit nearest Aadhaar enrollment center.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Update Address Online Change
content:
Aadhaar update address online change address in Aadhaar card
Step 1: Visit https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Login with Aadhaar number and OTP sent to registered mobile.
Step 3: Click on Update Aadhaar.
Step 4: Select Address as the field to update.
Step 5: Enter your new address carefully.
Step 6: Upload a valid address proof — electricity bill, bank passbook, or rent agreement.
Step 7: Pay fee of Rs 50 online.
Step 8: Note the URN Update Request Number to track status.
Update reflects in 5 to 7 working days.
Track status at https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using URN.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Update Mobile Number Phone Change
content:
Aadhaar update mobile number phone change register new mobile number
Mobile number update in Aadhaar CANNOT be done online. You must visit an Aadhaar Enrollment Center in person.
Step 1: Find nearest Aadhaar center at https://appointments.uidai.gov.in/easearch.aspx
Step 2: Book appointment or walk in directly.
Step 3: Carry original Aadhaar card and any valid ID proof.
Step 4: Request Mobile Number Update at the counter.
Step 5: New mobile number will be verified with OTP on the spot.
Step 6: Pay Rs 50.
Step 7: Update done within 1 to 2 working days.
Why it matters: Mobile number is needed to download e-Aadhaar, update address online, and use all UIDAI services.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Update Rejected Failed Problem Issue Not Working Fix
content:
Aadhaar update rejected failed problem issue not working what to do fix error
Common reasons why Aadhaar update gets rejected and how to fix them:
1. Document not clear — Upload a clear scanned copy or photo of the document. Blurry or dark photos are rejected.
2. Address on document does not match — The address you entered must exactly match what is written on your uploaded document.
3. Document expired — Use a recent document not older than 3 months for bills. Bank passbook must have recent entries.
4. Wrong document type uploaded — Only upload documents from the accepted list: electricity bill, water bill, bank passbook, rent agreement, passport, voter ID.
5. Name mismatch — Your name on the document must match your name in Aadhaar records.
6. Mobile number not registered — If OTP is not coming you need to first update mobile number by visiting enrollment center.
What to do after rejection:
Step 1: Check rejection reason in your email or at https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using URN.
Step 2: Fix the issue — upload better document or correct address.
Step 3: Submit a fresh update request.
Step 4: If online keeps getting rejected, visit nearest Aadhaar enrollment center in person.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Link Bank Account Mobile Number Status Check
content:
Aadhaar link bank account status check lock unlock biometrics verify
Check Aadhaar enrollment status:
Step 1: Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Click Check Aadhaar Status.
Step 3: Enter 14-digit EID and date-time from your enrollment slip.
Step 4: Enter OTP and check status.
Link Aadhaar with bank account:
Visit your bank branch or net banking portal. Provide Aadhaar number for linking.
Lock Aadhaar biometrics to prevent misuse:
Step 1: Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Click Lock or Unlock Biometrics.
Step 3: Login and choose Lock.
Verify if mobile is linked to Aadhaar: Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in and click Verify Aadhaar.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar OTP Not Coming SMS Not Received Problem
content:
Aadhaar OTP not coming SMS not received problem issue mobile number
If OTP is not coming on your mobile for Aadhaar services:
Reason 1: Mobile number is not registered with Aadhaar.
Solution: Visit nearest Aadhaar enrollment center to update your mobile number. Cost Rs 50.
Reason 2: Wrong mobile number entered.
Solution: Enter the mobile number that was given at time of Aadhaar enrollment.
Reason 3: Network issue or SIM not active.
Solution: Try after some time or use a different network.
Reason 4: OTP expired — OTP is valid for only 10 minutes.
Solution: Click Resend OTP and try again immediately.
To check which mobile number is linked to your Aadhaar: Visit https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in and click Verify Email or Mobile.
If you have permanently lost access to registered mobile: Visit Aadhaar enrollment center in person with original Aadhaar and ID proof.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Card Lost Damaged Reprint Order
content:
Aadhaar card lost damaged reprint order new Aadhaar card
If your Aadhaar card is lost or damaged:
Option 1 — Download e-Aadhaar instantly for free:
Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in, click Download Aadhaar, enter Aadhaar number, verify OTP, download PDF. This is free and works immediately.
Option 2 — Order physical reprint of Aadhaar card by post:
Step 1: Go to https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Click Order Aadhaar Reprint.
Step 3: Enter Aadhaar number and verify OTP.
Step 4: Pay Rs 50 including speed post charges.
Step 5: Aadhaar card delivered by post in 5 to 7 working days.
Your Aadhaar number remains the same — only the card is reissued.
Helpline: 1947

title: Aadhaar Name Update Correction Date of Birth Change
content:
Aadhaar name update correction change date of birth gender update
To update name in Aadhaar online:
Step 1: Visit https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
Step 2: Login with Aadhaar and OTP.
Step 3: Click Update Aadhaar and select Name.
Step 4: Enter corrected name exactly as in your document.
Step 5: Upload proof — gazette notification, marriage certificate, or school certificate.
Step 6: Pay Rs 50.
Name can be updated only twice in a lifetime online.
To update Date of Birth in Aadhaar:
Date of birth can be updated only ONCE in a lifetime.
Upload birth certificate or 10th marksheet as proof.
If update is rejected or changes not allowed online, visit Aadhaar enrollment center in person.
Helpline: 1947

title: PAN Card Apply Online Instant e-PAN Free
content:
PAN card apply online instant e-PAN free how to get PAN card
Instant e-PAN is completely free and takes only 10 minutes. You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number.
Step 1: Visit https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal
Step 2: Under Quick Links click Instant e-PAN.
Step 3: Click Get New e-PAN.
Step 4: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number.
Step 5: Enter OTP sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number.
Step 6: Verify your details — name, date of birth, gender auto-filled from Aadhaar.
Step 7: Click Submit — PAN is allotted instantly.
Step 8: Download PDF e-PAN which is accepted everywhere.
Cost: FREE. Time: 10 minutes.
Important: One PAN per person only. Having two PANs is illegal and attracts penalty.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Physical Apply Post Delivery NSDL
content:
PAN card physical apply post delivery NSDL UTIITSL form 49A
To apply for physical PAN card delivered by post:
Step 1: Visit https://onlineservices.nsdl.com/paam/endUserRegisterContact.html
Step 2: Select New PAN — Indian Citizen Form 49A.
Step 3: Fill all personal details — name, date of birth, address.
Step 4: Upload photograph, signature, and identity proof documents.
Step 5: Pay fee of Rs 107 for Indian address delivery.
Step 6: PAN card delivered by post within 15 to 20 working days.
Alternatively apply through https://www.utiitsl.com
Documents needed: Aadhaar card, photograph, signature.
PAN Helpline: 020-27218080 or 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Link Aadhaar Income Tax Mandatory Penalty
content:
PAN card link Aadhaar income tax mandatory penalty how to link PAN with Aadhaar
Linking PAN with Aadhaar is mandatory for all Indian citizens.
Step 1: Go to https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal
Step 2: Under Quick Links click Link Aadhaar.
Step 3: Enter your PAN number and Aadhaar number.
Step 4: Enter your name exactly as on Aadhaar.
Step 5: Pay Rs 1000 penalty fee through e-Pay Tax on the income tax portal.
Step 6: After payment submit the link request.
Step 7: Linking completed within 4 to 5 working days.
Check if already linked: Go to incometax.gov.in, click Link Aadhaar Status, enter PAN and Aadhaar numbers.
Consequences of not linking: PAN becomes inoperative and attracts Rs 1000 penalty on use.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Lost Duplicate Reprint Download
content:
PAN card lost damaged duplicate reprint download how to get new PAN card
Option 1 — Download e-PAN instantly for free:
Go to https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal, click Instant e-PAN, enter Aadhaar, verify OTP, download PDF. Free and instant.
Option 2 — Get physical duplicate PAN card:
Step 1: Go to https://www.onlineservices.nsdl.com
Step 2: Select Reprint of PAN Card.
Step 3: Enter existing PAN number, Aadhaar number, and date of birth.
Step 4: Verify OTP.
Step 5: Pay Rs 107.
Step 6: Physical card delivered in 15 working days.
Your PAN number remains the same — only the card is reissued.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Name Correction Update Change Details
content:
PAN card name correction update change details date of birth address
To correct or update details on PAN card:
Step 1: Visit https://onlineservices.nsdl.com
Step 2: Select Changes or Correction in Existing PAN Data.
Step 3: Enter your existing PAN number.
Step 4: Fill in the corrected details — name, date of birth, address.
Step 5: Upload supporting document as proof of the corrected detail.
Step 6: Pay fee of Rs 107.
Step 7: Updated PAN card delivered by post in 15 to 20 working days.
Documents for name change: Marriage certificate or gazette notification.
Documents for DOB change: Birth certificate or 10th marksheet.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Not Received Delayed Track Status Check
content:
PAN card not received delayed track status check application status
Track PAN card application status:
Step 1: Go to https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pantan/StatusTrack.html
Step 2: Select Application Type — New PAN or Changes in PAN.
Step 3: Enter your 15-digit acknowledgement number from application receipt.
Step 4: Click Submit to see current status.
Status meanings:
Under Process — Application being reviewed by Income Tax Department.
Dispatch — PAN card has been printed and dispatched by post.
Delivered — Card delivered to your address.
If PAN card not received after 25 working days:
Call PAN helpline: 020-27218080 or 1800-180-1961.
Or raise grievance at https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card for Minor Child Below 18 Years
content:
PAN card for minor child below 18 years how to apply
PAN card can be applied for minors and children of any age.
Step 1: Go to https://onlineservices.nsdl.com
Step 2: Select New PAN — Indian Citizen Form 49A.
Step 3: Select Date of Birth of the minor child.
Step 4: Enter parent or guardian details.
Step 5: Upload child's birth certificate as date of birth proof.
Step 6: Upload parent or guardian's PAN and identity proof.
Step 7: Upload child's photograph.
Step 8: Pay Rs 107.
Note: When the minor turns 18, biometrics must be updated by submitting Form 49A for changes. A new photograph and signature will be required.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: PAN Card Two PAN Cards Surrender Surrender Duplicate
content:
Two PAN cards surrender duplicate PAN illegal penalty what to do
Having two PAN cards is illegal and attracts a penalty of Rs 10000 under Section 272B of Income Tax Act.
How to surrender extra PAN card:
Step 1: Go to https://onlineservices.nsdl.com
Step 2: Select Changes or Correction in Existing PAN Data.
Step 3: Enter the PAN you want to KEEP.
Step 4: In the form mention the duplicate PAN number to be surrendered.
Step 5: Submit with documents and pay fee of Rs 107.
Step 6: Income Tax Department will deactivate the surrendered PAN.
Alternatively visit nearest Income Tax office with both PAN cards.
You will receive confirmation once duplicate PAN is surrendered.
PAN Helpline: 1800-180-1961

title: Passport Apply Fresh New Application Steps
content:
Passport apply fresh new application how to get passport India steps
Step 1: Register on https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: Login and click Apply for Fresh Passport.
Step 3: Fill the application form with all personal details.
Step 4: Pay fee — Normal 36-page passport Rs 1500, Tatkal Rs 3500.
Step 5: Book appointment at nearest Passport Seva Kendra PSK.
Step 6: Visit PSK on appointment date with original documents.
Required documents: Aadhaar card mandatory, Birth certificate or 10th marksheet, 2 passport size photographs white background.
Step 7: Biometrics captured at PSK.
Step 8: Police verification at your home address.
Step 9: Normal takes 30 to 45 days, Tatkal takes 7 to 10 days.
Track status at https://passportindia.gov.in
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Tatkal Passport Urgent Fast Track Emergency
content:
Tatkal passport urgent fast track emergency quick passport delivery
Tatkal passport is fast-track service. Delivery in 7 to 10 days.
Fee: Rs 3500 for 36-page passport, Rs 4000 for 60-page.
Step 1: Apply at https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: Select Tatkal scheme while filling application form.
Step 3: Pay Tatkal fee online.
Step 4: Book PSK appointment — Tatkal slots fill up fast, book early morning.
Step 5: Visit PSK with documents: Aadhaar, PAN or Voter ID, Address proof, Birth certificate or 10th marksheet, 2 white background photos, Annexure F self-declaration from passportindia.gov.in.
Step 6: Police verification waived for most Tatkal applicants.
Step 7: Passport dispatched within 7 to 10 days.
Track at https://passportindia.gov.in
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Renewal Reissue Expired Damaged
content:
Passport renewal reissue expired damaged old passport how to renew
Step 1: Login at https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: Click Apply for Re-issue of Passport.
Step 3: Select reason — Expired, Lost, Damaged, or Change of details.
Step 4: Fill form with updated details.
Step 5: Pay fee — Rs 1500 Normal, Rs 3500 Tatkal renewal.
Step 6: Book PSK appointment.
Step 7: Visit PSK with old passport, Aadhaar, and 2 passport photos.
Expired less than 3 years: Simplified renewal process.
Expired more than 3 years: Full fresh application required.
Track at https://passportindia.gov.in
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Track Status Application Police Verification
content:
Passport track application status check police verification pending
Track passport application status:
Step 1: Go to https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: Click Track Application Status.
Step 3: Enter File Number from acknowledgement receipt.
Step 4: Enter Date of Birth and click Track.
Track by SMS: Send SPEED followed by file number to 9704100100.
Application stages:
Application Received — your application submitted.
Payment Confirmed — payment verified.
Appointment Scheduled — PSK visit booked.
Police Verification Initiated — police checking your address.
Police Verification Completed — police report submitted.
Passport Printed — your passport is printed.
Dispatched — passport sent by Speed Post.
If police verification is pending for more than 21 days contact your nearest police station.
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Lost Stolen Police Complaint FIR
content:
Passport lost stolen police complaint FIR what to do report
If your passport is lost or stolen:
Step 1: File an FIR at nearest police station immediately. Get a copy of the FIR.
Step 2: Go to https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 3: Apply for Re-issue of Passport.
Step 4: Select reason as Lost Passport.
Step 5: Upload copy of FIR as supporting document.
Step 6: Pay fee of Rs 3000 for lost passport application.
Step 7: Book PSK appointment and appear in person.
Step 8: New passport issued after verification. Lost passport is permanently invalidated.
If passport lost abroad: Contact nearest Indian Embassy or Consulate immediately.
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Rejected Rejected Application Reason How to Fix
content:
Passport rejected application rejected reason why fix reapply
Common reasons for passport rejection:
1. Documents mismatch — Name or date of birth on documents does not match application form. Fix by correcting the form.
2. Incomplete application — All fields not filled. Recheck and complete the form.
3. Bad photograph — Photo not meeting specifications. Use white background, recent, clear face photo.
4. Police verification failed — Adverse police report. Contact your local police station for clarification.
5. Criminal case pending — Passport withheld pending court clearance.
How to reapply after rejection:
Step 1: Check rejection reason in your email or at passportindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Correct the issue.
Step 3: Submit a fresh application with corrected details and documents.
Step 4: Pay fresh application fee.
If passport rejected due to police verification issue, file a grievance at passportindia.gov.in under Grievances section.
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Documents Required List Checklist
content:
Passport documents required list checklist what to bring PSK
Documents required for passport application at PSK:
Mandatory for all: Aadhaar card original and photocopy, 2 recent passport size photographs with white background.
Proof of Date of Birth any one: Birth certificate, 10th class marksheet, School leaving certificate, PAN card.
Proof of Address any one: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Driving License, Electricity bill, Bank passbook, Rent agreement.
For Tatkal additionally: Annexure F self-declaration form downloaded from passportindia.gov.in.
For minors under 18: Parents passports or Aadhaar, Parents declaration form Annexure H.
For government employees: NOC from employer — Annexure B.
For name change after marriage: Marriage certificate.
All documents must be originals. Photocopies needed alongside originals.
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Passport Appointment Book Reschedule Cancel PSK
content:
Passport appointment book reschedule cancel PSK Passport Seva Kendra
Book PSK appointment:
Step 1: Login at https://passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: Click Schedule Appointment.
Step 3: Select your nearest PSK from the list.
Step 4: Select available date and time slot.
Step 5: Confirm appointment and note appointment number.
Reschedule appointment:
Login and go to View Saved or Submitted Applications, click Reschedule Appointment.
Cancel appointment:
Login and go to View Saved Applications, click Cancel Appointment.
Tips for getting appointment faster:
Log in at exactly 8:00 AM — new slots are released daily at 8 AM.
Try alternative nearby PSK if your preferred center is full.
Try booking 2 to 3 weeks in advance.
Helpline: 1800-258-1800

title: Voter ID New Registration Apply Form 6
content:
Voter ID new registration apply how to register as voter Form 6 EPIC card
Step 1: Visit https://voters.eci.gov.in
Step 2: Click Register as New Voter — opens Form 6.
Step 3: Enter name, date of birth, address, mobile number, email.
Step 4: Upload photograph and address proof.
Step 5: Submit the form.
Step 6: BLO Booth Level Officer verifies your details at home address.
Step 7: EPIC Voter ID card dispatched within 30 days.
Eligibility: Indian citizen, 18 years or above, resident of the constituency.
Documents needed: Photograph, Age proof, Address proof.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: Voter ID Download e-EPIC Digital Voter Card
content:
Voter ID download e-EPIC digital voter card online how to download
Step 1: Go to https://voters.eci.gov.in
Step 2: Click e-EPIC Download.
Step 3: Login with EPIC number and OTP, or mobile number and OTP.
Step 4: Verify details.
Step 5: Click Download — PDF saved to device.
e-EPIC is legally valid at banks, courts, airports, and during elections.
Find your EPIC number: Go to https://electoralsearch.eci.gov.in and search by your name and constituency.
Not in voter list? Register at voters.eci.gov.in using Form 6.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: Voter ID Name Correction Update Address Change
content:
Voter ID name correction update address change details Form 8
To correct or update details in Voter ID card:
Step 1: Go to https://voters.eci.gov.in
Step 2: Click Fill Form 8 — for corrections in electoral roll.
Step 3: Enter your EPIC number.
Step 4: Select what needs correction — name, address, photo, date of birth.
Step 5: Upload supporting document as proof.
Step 6: Submit form.
Step 7: BLO verifies and correction is done within 30 days.
Updated voter ID card sent by post or downloadable as e-EPIC.
To change address to new constituency: Fill Form 8A for transfer of voter registration.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: Voter ID Check Status Enrollment Number Search Name
content:
Voter ID check status enrollment number search name in voter list
Check if your name is in voter list:
Step 1: Go to https://electoralsearch.eci.gov.in
Step 2: Search by Name — enter your name, state, district, and constituency.
Step 3: Or search by EPIC number — enter your voter ID card number.
Step 4: Your voter details and polling booth will be shown.
Track Voter ID card application status:
Go to https://voters.eci.gov.in, click Track Application Status, enter reference ID from your application.
Check polling booth location: electoralsearch.eci.gov.in shows your exact booth address and booth number.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: Voter ID Not Received Lost Damaged Reprint
content:
Voter ID not received lost damaged reprint how to get duplicate voter card
If Voter ID card is lost or damaged or not received:
Option 1 — Download e-EPIC for free online:
Go to https://voters.eci.gov.in, click e-EPIC Download, login with EPIC number and OTP, download PDF immediately.
Option 2 — Request physical reprint:
Step 1: Go to voters.eci.gov.in or visit BLO or ERO office.
Step 2: Fill request for duplicate EPIC card.
Step 3: Submit with Aadhaar and a photograph.
Step 4: New physical card sent by post in 30 days.
Note: Downloading e-EPIC is free and immediate. Physical reprint takes time.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: Voter ID Deleted Name Removed from Voter List
content:
Voter ID deleted name removed from voter list how to add back re-register
If your name is removed from voter list or deleted:
Step 1: Check at https://electoralsearch.eci.gov.in if your name is actually removed.
Step 2: Visit your local BLO Booth Level Officer or Electoral Registration Office ERO.
Step 3: File an objection using Form 7A to challenge deletion.
Step 4: Or re-register as fresh voter using Form 6 at voters.eci.gov.in
Step 5: Submit all required documents — address proof, age proof, photograph.
Names are typically removed during electoral roll revision if voter has shifted address or is deceased. Re-registration is straightforward.
Election Helpline: 1950

title: GST New Registration Apply Online GSTIN
content:
GST new registration apply online GSTIN how to register for GST
GST registration required if annual turnover exceeds Rs 20 lakh (Rs 10 lakh for special category states).
Step 1: Visit https://www.gst.gov.in
Step 2: Go to Services then Registration then New Registration.
Step 3: Fill Part A — Enter PAN, email, mobile. Verify OTP. Note TRN Temporary Reference Number.
Step 4: Fill Part B using TRN — Enter business name, address, bank details, business type.
Step 5: Upload documents — PAN card, Aadhaar, address proof of business, bank statement or cancelled cheque.
Step 6: Submit application.
Step 7: GSTIN issued within 7 working days.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: GST File Returns GSTR-1 GSTR-3B Monthly Annual
content:
GST file returns GSTR-1 GSTR-3B monthly annual how to file GST return
Types of GST returns:
GSTR-1: Monthly sales details due on 11th of next month.
GSTR-3B: Monthly summary and tax payment due on 20th of next month.
GSTR-9: Annual return filed once a year.
Steps to file GSTR-3B:
Step 1: Login at https://www.gst.gov.in
Step 2: Go to Services then Returns then Returns Dashboard.
Step 3: Select financial year and return period.
Step 4: Fill GSTR-1 first with all sales invoices.
Step 5: Fill GSTR-3B — check auto-filled purchase data and pay any tax due.
Step 6: File using DSC or EVC.
Late filing penalty: Rs 50 per day minimum Rs 200.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: GST Login Password Forgot Reset Problem
content:
GST login password forgot reset problem issue not working
If unable to login to GST portal or forgot password:
Reset password:
Step 1: Go to https://www.gst.gov.in
Step 2: Click Login then Forgot Username or Forgot Password.
Step 3: Enter your GSTIN number.
Step 4: Verify with OTP sent to registered mobile or email.
Step 5: Set new password.
If registered mobile number changed:
File a request at GST helpdesk with supporting documents.
If GSTIN suspended or cancelled:
Go to gst.gov.in and file Form GST REG-21 for revocation of cancellation.
GST portal down or technical issues: Try after some time or clear browser cache. Use Chrome or Firefox browser.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: GST Cancellation Surrender Close Business
content:
GST cancellation surrender close business deregister GSTIN
To cancel GST registration when closing business:
Step 1: Login to https://www.gst.gov.in
Step 2: Go to Services then Registration then Application for Cancellation of Registration.
Step 3: Select reason for cancellation — Business discontinued, Transfer of business, etc.
Step 4: Enter date from which cancellation is required.
Step 5: Fill in stock and liability details.
Step 6: Submit application.
GST officer may verify and approve cancellation within 30 days.
File all pending returns before applying for cancellation.
Pay all pending tax dues before cancellation.
After cancellation: File final return GSTR-10 within 3 months.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: GST Invoice Rules Tax Calculation HSN SAC
content:
GST invoice rules tax calculation HSN SAC code how to calculate GST
GST Invoice requirements:
Every GST registered business must issue a proper tax invoice with: GSTIN of supplier, Date of invoice, Invoice number, Name and address of buyer, HSN or SAC code, Quantity, Rate, Taxable value, GST rate and amount, Total amount.
GST tax rates: 0% — essential items like milk, vegetables. 5% — household items. 12% — processed food. 18% — most goods and services. 28% — luxury items.
GST calculation: Tax amount = Taxable value multiplied by GST rate divided by 100.
For intrastate supply: CGST and SGST each at half the rate.
For interstate supply: IGST at full rate.
HSN codes for goods, SAC codes for services are mandatory on invoices.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: GST Refund Claim Process Input Tax Credit ITC
content:
GST refund claim process input tax credit ITC how to claim
To claim GST refund:
Step 1: Login to https://www.gst.gov.in
Step 2: Go to Services then Refunds then Application for Refund.
Step 3: Select refund type — Excess cash balance, ITC on exports, etc.
Step 4: Fill in refund amount and details.
Step 5: Upload supporting documents — invoices, bank statements.
Step 6: Submit — refund processed within 60 days.
Input Tax Credit ITC:
ITC allows you to reduce GST paid on purchases from GST collected on sales.
Claim ITC in GSTR-3B under Section 4.
ITC available only if supplier has filed GSTR-1 and invoice appears in your GSTR-2B.
GST Helpdesk: 1800-103-4786

title: Driving License Learner License Apply Online Parivahan
content:
Driving license learner license apply online parivahan how to get learner license
Step 1: Go to https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan
Step 2: Click Driving Licence Services and select your State.
Step 3: Click Apply for Learner Licence.
Step 4: Fill form with personal details.
Step 5: Choose vehicle class — MCWG motorcycle with gear, LMV car, MCWOG scooter.
Step 6: Upload Aadhaar as identity and address proof, 10th marksheet as age proof, passport photo and signature.
Step 7: Pay Rs 200 online.
Step 8: Book theory test at nearest RTO.
Step 9: Pass computer-based theory test — 20 MCQ questions, pass mark 12 out of 20. Topics: road signs, traffic rules, safety.
Learner License valid for 6 months. Apply for permanent DL after 30 days.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Permanent Driving License Apply Test Parivahan
content:
Permanent driving license apply test how to get permanent DL after learner license
After getting Learner License wait minimum 30 days then apply for Permanent DL.
Step 1: Apply on https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan
Step 2: Select Driving License Services and your state.
Step 3: Click Apply for Driving Licence.
Step 4: Enter Learner License number.
Step 5: Pay Rs 300 to 500 and book practical driving test at RTO.
Step 6: Appear for driving test at RTO with your vehicle.
Step 7: Pass the practical driving test.
Step 8: Permanent Driving License card sent by post within 7 days.
Practical test includes: Vehicle control, Road signs, Lane discipline, Parking, U-turn.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Driving License Renewal Expired Renew Online
content:
Driving license renewal expired renew online how to renew DL
To renew Driving License online:
Step 1: Go to https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan
Step 2: Click Driving Licence Services and select your state.
Step 3: Click Renewal of Driving Licence.
Step 4: Enter DL number and date of birth.
Step 5: Upload current photo and signature.
Step 6: Upload medical certificate Form 1A if age above 40 or for commercial vehicles.
Step 7: Pay renewal fee Rs 200 to 400.
Step 8: DL renewed and sent by post in 7 days.
Can renew online up to 1 year after expiry.
After 1 year of expiry: Must visit RTO in person.
DL must be renewed 30 days before expiry date.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Driving License Lost Damaged Duplicate Apply
content:
Driving license lost damaged duplicate apply how to get duplicate DL
If Driving License is lost or damaged:
Step 1: File FIR at nearest police station for lost DL and get FIR copy.
Step 2: Go to https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan
Step 3: Select Driving Licence Services and your state.
Step 4: Click Issue of Duplicate Driving Licence.
Step 5: Enter your DL number and date of birth.
Step 6: Upload FIR copy for lost DL or damage photo for damaged DL.
Step 7: Pay duplicate DL fee of Rs 200 to 300.
Step 8: Duplicate DL card sent by post within 7 days.
Can also apply at RTO office in person with same documents.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Driving License Address Change Name Correction Update
content:
Driving license address change name correction update details
To change address or correct name on Driving License:
Step 1: Go to https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan
Step 2: Select your state and click Change of Address in DL.
Step 3: Enter DL number and verify with OTP.
Step 4: Enter new address details.
Step 5: Upload new address proof — Aadhaar, electricity bill, bank passbook.
Step 6: Pay fee of Rs 200 to 300.
Step 7: Updated DL card sent by post in 7 days.
For name correction — visit RTO office in person with gazette notification or marriage certificate.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Driving License Documents Required Checklist
content:
Driving license documents required checklist what to bring RTO
Documents for Learner License:
Age proof any one: Aadhaar card, Birth certificate, 10th marksheet, Passport.
Address proof any one: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Passport, Electricity bill.
Identity proof: Aadhaar card preferred.
Passport size photographs: 2 recent photos.
Documents for Permanent Driving License:
All above documents plus: Original Learner License card.
Documents for Renewal:
Old DL card, Aadhaar, medical certificate if required, passport photo.
For commercial vehicle license additionally: Medical fitness certificate Form 1.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: Driving License Test Failed Rejected What to Do
content:
Driving license test failed rejected what to do reappear retake
If you fail the driving license test:
For Learner License theory test:
You can reappear after 7 days. Re-book test at parivahan.gov.in or at RTO.
Study road signs, traffic rules, and safety guidelines before retaking.
Pay fresh test fee of Rs 50 to Rs 100.
For Permanent DL practical driving test:
You can reappear after 7 days. Re-book practical test slot.
Practice vehicle control, parking, lane discipline, and U-turns before retesting.
Pay fresh test fee.
No limit on number of attempts for both tests.
Learner License expires after 6 months — must get permanent DL before expiry.
Helpline: 1800-120-1553

title: DigiLocker Create Account Setup How to Use
content:
DigiLocker create account setup how to use register sign up
DigiLocker is free government app to store and access all documents digitally.
Step 1: Visit https://www.digilocker.gov.in or download DigiLocker app from Play Store or App Store.
Step 2: Click Sign Up.
Step 3: Enter mobile number and verify with OTP.
Step 4: Create 6-digit security PIN — remember this PIN.
Step 5: Optionally link Aadhaar for more documents.
Step 6: Account ready with 1GB free secure cloud storage.
Access documents after signup:
Go to Issued Documents, click plus button, search for document issuer and fetch.
Available: Aadhaar from UIDAI, PAN from Income Tax, DL and RC from Transport, 10th 12th marksheets from CBSE, Degree from university.
DigiLocker documents legally valid under IT Act 2000.
Helpline: 1800-891-3000

title: DigiLocker Download Documents Aadhaar PAN Marksheet Degree
content:
DigiLocker download documents Aadhaar PAN marksheet degree certificate
How to download documents from DigiLocker:
Step 1: Login to https://www.digilocker.gov.in or app.
Step 2: Click Issued Documents.
Step 3: Click the plus icon to add new document.
Step 4: Search for the issuing organization — for example CBSE, UIDAI, Income Tax.
Step 5: Enter your details like roll number, PAN, Aadhaar.
Step 6: Document is fetched and saved to your DigiLocker.
Step 7: Click on document to view or download PDF.
Popular documents available:
Aadhaar: Issued by UIDAI. PAN card: Issued by Income Tax Department. Driving License: Issued by Transport Department. Class 10 and 12 marksheets: CBSE, ICSE, or state boards. Degree and diploma: University Grants Commission linked universities.
Helpline: 1800-891-3000

title: DigiLocker Login Problem Password Forgot PIN Reset
content:
DigiLocker login problem password forgot PIN reset issue not working
If unable to login to DigiLocker:
Forgot PIN:
Step 1: Open DigiLocker and click Forgot PIN.
Step 2: Enter mobile number and verify with OTP.
Step 3: Set new 6-digit PIN.
Mobile number changed or lost access:
Step 1: Open DigiLocker and click Login with Aadhaar instead.
Step 2: Enter Aadhaar number and OTP on Aadhaar-linked mobile.
Step 3: After login update mobile number in settings.
App not working or documents not loading:
Clear app cache and try again. Update app to latest version. Try on browser at digilocker.gov.in instead.
Document not available in DigiLocker:
Not all issuers are on DigiLocker. Check if your institution is listed.
Helpline: 1800-891-3000

title: DigiLocker Share Documents Upload Self Documents
content:
DigiLocker share documents upload self uploaded documents validity
Share DigiLocker documents:
Step 1: Login and open the document.
Step 2: Click Share icon.
Step 3: Share via email, WhatsApp, or generate QR code.
Recipient can verify document using QR code or at digilocker.gov.in.
Upload self-documents:
You can upload your own documents in Drive section — PDF, JPEG up to 10MB per file.
Self-uploaded documents are NOT digitally signed and have lower legal validity than issued documents.
Issued documents from government are more valid — use those for official purposes.
DigiLocker documents accepted at: Banks, Railways, Income Tax, Courts, Airports, Insurance companies, and most government offices.
Helpline: 1800-891-3000

title: DigiLocker Not Showing Document Not Found Unavailable
content:
DigiLocker document not showing not found unavailable error problem
If document is not available or showing error in DigiLocker:
Aadhaar not showing: Link Aadhaar to DigiLocker in settings first. Then fetch from UIDAI.
PAN not showing: Fetch PAN from Income Tax Department. Enter PAN number when prompted.
DL or RC not showing: Some states are not yet fully integrated. Check parivahan.gov.in directly.
Marksheet not showing: CBSE marksheets are available for 2004 onwards. For older marksheets contact your board directly.
Degree not available: Not all universities are on DigiLocker. Check National Academic Depository for degree certificates.
Document showing wrong details: Contact the issuing authority — UIDAI for Aadhaar, IT department for PAN.
Helpline: 1800-891-3000

title: Ration Card Apply New How to Get State Process
content:
Ration card apply new how to get state process documents
Ration card application handled by State governments. Process varies by state.
Step 1: Visit State Food Department website — search your state name ration card apply online.
Step 2: Or visit nearest Tehsil office or Block office in person.
Step 3: Fill the Ration Card application form.
Step 4: Submit required documents — Aadhaar of all family members, electricity bill as address proof, income certificate from Tehsil, family photograph.
Step 5: Receive acknowledgement receipt with application number.
Step 6: Field officer visits home to verify.
Step 7: Ration card issued within 30 to 45 days.
Check national ration card status at https://nfsa.gov.in
Types: AAY for poorest families, BPL Below Poverty Line, APL Above Poverty Line.
Food Helpline: 1800-111-001

title: Ration Card Documents Required Eligibility
content:
Ration card documents required eligibility who can apply
Eligibility for ration card:
AAY card: Poorest of poor families, destitute households, households headed by widow or disabled person.
BPL card: Families below poverty line. Income below Rs 10000 per month typically.
APL card: Families above poverty line.
Documents required:
Aadhaar card of all family members including children.
Proof of residence: Electricity bill, Water bill, Rent agreement.
Income certificate from Tehsil or Block office.
Family photograph — all members together.
Caste certificate if applying for SC or ST category.
No existing ration card — surrender old card if migrating from other state.
Food Helpline: 1800-111-001

title: Ration Card Add Name Member Family New Born
content:
Ration card add name member family new born baby how to add member
To add a new family member to existing ration card:
Step 1: Visit Tehsil or Food Supply Office.
Step 2: Fill application form for addition of member.
Step 3: Attach documents: Birth certificate for newborn, Marriage certificate for new spouse, Aadhaar of new member, Existing ration card.
Step 4: Submit form with documents.
Step 5: Officer verifies and adds member within 15 to 30 days.
For online addition: Visit your state Food Department portal and look for Add Member option.
For newborn baby: Can be added within 6 months of birth.
For marriage: Spouse can be added or new card can be issued after deletion from parents card.
Food Helpline: 1800-111-001

title: Ration Card Not Working Rejected Cancelled Suspended
content:
Ration card not working rejected cancelled suspended problem fix
Common ration card problems and solutions:
Ration card cancelled or suspended:
Visit Tehsil office with Aadhaar and income proof. Request for reinstatement.
Common reasons: Aadhaar not seeded, duplicate card detected, income exceeded limit.
Not getting ration items:
Check if Aadhaar is linked to ration card at nfsa.gov.in. Link Aadhaar at fair price shop or Tehsil office.
Fair price shop not giving proper amount:
File complaint at state food department helpline or visit District Supply Officer.
Ration card not working at e-PoS machine:
Try fingerprint of different family member. Machine may need maintenance — visit next day.
If Aadhaar seeding issue: Visit CSC center or Tehsil office with Aadhaar for re-seeding.
Food Helpline: 1800-111-001

title: Ration Card Transfer Surrender Migration New State
content:
Ration card transfer surrender migration new state how to transfer
If moving to a new state or district:
Step 1: Surrender existing ration card at current Tehsil or Food Supply Office.
Step 2: Get surrender certificate from the office.
Step 3: Apply for new ration card in new state with surrender certificate, Aadhaar, address proof of new home, family photograph.
Step 4: New ration card issued within 30 to 45 days.
Transfer within same state:
Step 1: Visit Tehsil and fill address change form.
Step 2: Attach new address proof.
Step 3: Card transferred to new fair price shop in 15 days.
One Nation One Ration Card ONORC: If you have ration card from any state, you can use it at any fair price shop across India. No need to transfer card for temporary migration.
Food Helpline: 1800-111-001

title: PM Kisan Apply Registration Status Check Payment
content:
PM Kisan Samman Nidhi apply registration status check payment installment
PM Kisan gives Rs 6000 per year to eligible farmer families in 3 installments of Rs 2000 each.
Eligibility: Small and marginal farmer families with landholding up to 2 hectares.
How to register:
Step 1: Go to https://pmkisan.gov.in
Step 2: Click Farmer Corner then New Farmer Registration.
Step 3: Enter Aadhaar number and select state.
Step 4: Fill details — name, address, bank account, land records.
Step 5: Submit and wait for state verification.
Check payment status:
Step 1: Go to https://pmkisan.gov.in
Step 2: Click Beneficiary Status under Farmer Corner.
Step 3: Enter Aadhaar number or mobile or bank account.
Step 4: Click Get Data — all installment payments shown.
PM Kisan Helpline: 155261 or 1800-115-526

title: PM Kisan Payment Not Received Failed Rejected eKYC
content:
PM Kisan payment not received failed rejected eKYC how to fix installment
If PM Kisan payment is not coming or rejected:
Reason 1: eKYC not done.
Fix: Go to pmkisan.gov.in, click eKYC under Farmer Corner, enter Aadhaar and verify OTP. Or visit nearest CSC center.
Reason 2: Bank account not linked to Aadhaar.
Fix: Visit your bank branch and request Aadhaar seeding. Bring Aadhaar and bank passbook.
Reason 3: Land records not verified.
Fix: Contact your local Patwari or Tehsildar to verify land records.
Reason 4: Duplicate or wrong Aadhaar number entered.
Fix: Contact PM Kisan helpline or visit nearest CSC center.
Reason 5: Beneficiary in excluded category.
Government employees, income tax payers, institutional landholders are excluded.
PM Kisan Helpline: 155261

title: Ayushman Bharat Health Card Apply PMJAY
content:
Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY health card apply how to get free treatment PMJAY
Ayushman Bharat provides free health insurance Rs 5 lakh per family per year at empanelled hospitals.
Check eligibility:
Step 1: Go to https://pmjay.gov.in
Step 2: Click Am I Eligible.
Step 3: Enter mobile and Aadhaar to check.
Get Ayushman card online:
Step 1: Go to https://beneficiary.nha.gov.in
Step 2: Login and generate Ayushman card.
Get card at hospital:
Visit any empanelled hospital. Go to Ayushman Mitra at help desk. They verify and generate card free.
Get at CSC center: Visit nearest CSC at csc.gov.in — Rs 30 service fee.
Benefits: Rs 5 lakh free cashless treatment per family per year at 25000 hospitals across India.
Ayushman Helpline: 14555

title: Ayushman Bharat Hospital Not Accepting Card Problem
content:
Ayushman Bharat hospital not accepting card problem rejected treatment denied
If hospital is not accepting Ayushman Bharat card:
Step 1: Check if hospital is empanelled at https://hospitals.pmjay.gov.in
Step 2: If hospital is listed, show them your Ayushman card and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Ask to see the Ayushman Mitra — every empanelled hospital must have one.
Step 4: If hospital still refuses, call Ayushman helpline 14555 immediately.
Step 5: File complaint at pmjay.gov.in under Grievance section.
If your Ayushman card is not working:
Update Aadhaar in Ayushman card at beneficiary.nha.gov.in or CSC center.
Check if family is in SECC 2011 database — visit your nearest government hospital.
Ayushman Helpline: 14555

title: MUDRA Loan Apply Business Loan Pradhan Mantri
content:
MUDRA loan apply business loan Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana how to get
MUDRA loans for small businesses up to Rs 10 lakh.
Types: Shishu up to Rs 50000, Kishor Rs 50001 to Rs 5 lakh, Tarun Rs 5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh.
How to apply:
Step 1: Visit any bank, NBFC, or MFI — SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda, Canara Bank.
Step 2: Ask for PM Mudra Loan form.
Step 3: Submit business plan, Aadhaar, PAN, address proof, bank statements.
Step 4: Loan approved based on business viability.
Apply online through https://www.udyamimitra.in
No collateral required for Shishu and Kishor loans.
Purpose: Manufacturing, Trading, Service sector small businesses.
MUDRA Helpline: 1800-180-1111

title: PM Awas Yojana Apply Housing Loan Subsidy Urban Rural
content:
PM Awas Yojana apply housing loan subsidy urban rural how to apply
PM Awas Yojana provides home loan interest subsidy to eligible families.
Eligibility: Annual income EWS up to Rs 3 lakh, LIG up to Rs 6 lakh, MIG-1 up to Rs 12 lakh, MIG-2 up to Rs 18 lakh.
Apply Urban:
Step 1: Go to https://pmaymis.gov.in
Step 2: Click Citizen Assessment.
Step 3: Choose category and fill Aadhaar details.
Step 4: Fill application with income and housing details.
Step 5: Submit and note application ID.
Step 6: Apply for home loan at any bank — subsidy credited directly to loan.
Track application at https://pmaymis.gov.in using application ID.
PM Awas Helpline: 1800-11-3377

title: Ujjwala Yojana LPG Gas Connection Apply Free
content:
Ujjwala Yojana LPG gas connection apply free how to get
PM Ujjwala Yojana provides free LPG gas connection to BPL families.
Eligibility: Women from BPL Below Poverty Line households, SECC 2011 list, SC or ST families, forest dwellers, tea garden workers.
How to apply:
Step 1: Visit nearest LPG distributor of any company — Indane, HP Gas, or Bharat Gas.
Step 2: Fill KYC form and Ujjwala application form.
Step 3: Submit documents: Aadhaar card, BPL card or ration card, Bank account details, Address proof.
Step 4: Gas connection provided free of charge.
Step 5: Get subsidy credited to bank account for each refill.
Check eligibility online at https://pmuy.gov.in
Ujjwala Helpline: 1906

title: Jan Dhan Account Zero Balance Bank Account Open
content:
Jan Dhan account zero balance bank account open Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
PM Jan Dhan Yojana provides zero balance bank accounts to all Indians.
Benefits: Zero balance account, RuPay debit card, Rs 2 lakh accident insurance, Rs 30000 life insurance, Overdraft up to Rs 10000, Direct benefit transfer for government schemes.
How to open Jan Dhan account:
Step 1: Visit any bank branch — SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda, post office, or regional rural bank.
Step 2: Ask for PM Jan Dhan Yojana account opening form.
Step 3: Fill form with name, address, date of birth.
Step 4: Submit Aadhaar card as KYC document.
Step 5: Account opened on the same day.
Step 6: RuPay debit card issued within 7 days.
PMJDY Helpline: 1800-11-0001

title: Sukanya Samriddhi Girl Child Savings Account
content:
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana girl child savings account how to open
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is a savings scheme for girl children with high interest rate of 8.2% per year.
Eligibility: Girl child below 10 years of age. Parents or guardian can open account.
How to open:
Step 1: Visit nearest Post Office or bank — SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda, etc.
Step 2: Fill Sukanya Samriddhi account opening form.
Step 3: Submit documents: Girl child birth certificate, Parent or guardian Aadhaar, Parent or guardian PAN, Address proof.
Step 4: Deposit minimum Rs 250 to open account.
Features: Minimum deposit Rs 250 per year, Maximum Rs 1.5 lakh per year, Tax exempt under Section 80C, Maturity after 21 years from opening, Partial withdrawal for education after girl turns 18.
Helpline: 1800-11-0001

title: APY Atal Pension Yojana NPS National Pension
content:
APY Atal Pension Yojana NPS National Pension System how to join apply
Atal Pension Yojana provides guaranteed monthly pension of Rs 1000 to Rs 5000 after age 60.
Eligibility: Indian citizen aged 18 to 40 years with bank account.
How to join:
Step 1: Visit your bank branch or net banking portal.
Step 2: Ask for APY enrollment form or go to APY section in net banking.
Step 3: Choose pension amount — Rs 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, or 5000 per month.
Step 4: Monthly contribution auto-debited from bank account based on age and chosen pension.
Contribution example: If 18 years old and want Rs 5000 pension, pay Rs 210 per month. If 35 years old, pay Rs 902 per month.
Government co-contributes 50% or Rs 1000 whichever is lower for new subscribers.
Helpline: 1800-110-708
